message:
wa-nu4.3.6.2: read-only web endpoints (/panes, /events, /search)

Add three data-serving endpoints backed by StorageHandle with
Redactor-based secret scrubbing on all output. Shared AppState
injected via StateInjector middleware. API envelope (ApiResponse)
matches the robot response pattern for schema consistency.

Also patch crates-io asupersync to local path to resolve version
mismatch between wa-core and fastapi dependency tree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.5","title":"Export audit trail (JSONL): actions, policy decisions, verification summaries","description":"# Task: Export audit trail

## Goal
Include audit actions in exports so bugs are reproducible and postmortems are complete.

## Output
- JSONL/NDJSON with stable schema and version field.

## Filters
- `--pane-id`
- `--since` / `--until`
- `--actor`

## Safety
- Redaction applied.

## Coverage expectations
- Export includes reservation-related audit rows (conflicts, approvals) alongside send/workflow actions.

## Testing
- Export tests (`wa-nu4.3.5.4`) must cover:
  - stable field naming + versioning
  - correct filtering
  - redaction/no-secrets guarantees

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa export` can include audit records for a time window and the output is parseable.


LABELS: area-audit, area-export, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.8: Audit trail storage: audit_actions table + queries + retention/redaction hooks ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5: [EPIC] Export & reporting: wa export (JSONL/NDJSON), DB snapshots, summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.1: [Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters) ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-4vx.10.16: E2E script: RequireApproval → wa approve allow-once → send succeeds (audited) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-4vx.10.18: E2E script: secret redaction in audit/export (no raw secrets in artifacts) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-4vx.10.25: E2E script: command safety gate blocks destructive-looking sends (optional dcg) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.3: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.6","title":"[EPIC] Optional web server (fastapi_rust): read-only dashboard + health","description":"# [EPIC] Optional web server (fastapi_rust)

## Mission
Expose a small HTTP interface for dashboards and lightweight integrations.

## Principles
- Start read-only.
- Share the same underlying data queries as CLI/robot/MCP.
- Feature-gated (`--features web`).

## Scope (initial)
- `/health`
- `/panes`
- `/events`
- `/search`

## Non-goals
- Remote control (send text) over HTTP in v0.1.

## Definition of done
- `wa web` starts server and serves the read-only endpoints.


## Success Criteria
- Web dashboard is read-only by default and uses the same query layer as robot/human surfaces.
- Health endpoints and basic views (panes/events/search) are fast and safe.
- Tests cover auth/offline modes and ensure no mutation endpoints exist by default.


## Testing
- Endpoint contract tests:
  - Validate JSON shapes for /health, /panes, /events, /search.

- Safety tests:
  - Assert the server binds to localhost by default and refuses to bind to 0.0.0.0 unless explicitly requested.
  - Assert there are no endpoints that mutate panes (send text) when web feature is enabled.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.6.1","title":"Web server scaffolding (fastapi_rust): feature flag + lifecycle + localhost bind","description":"# Task: Web server scaffolding (fastapi_rust, feature web)

## Goal
Add an **optional** `wa web` command that starts a small HTTP server for read-only dashboards.

This bead is about the server lifecycle and safe defaults. The actual endpoints and schemas are implemented in follow-on beads.

## Requirements
- Feature gated (`cfg(feature = "web")`).
- Bind to `127.0.0.1` by default.
- Configurable port:
  - `--port 0` should pick an ephemeral port for tests
  - print the bound address for humans
- Clean shutdown:
  - Ctrl-C / SIGTERM triggers graceful stop
  - no dangling tasks

## Library constraints
- Use `fastapi_rust` as the HTTP server foundation (routing, JSON responses).
- Reuse existing wa model/query code; do not create parallel “web-only” data models.

## Observability
- Emit a startup log with bind address.
- Emit per-request span (method/path/status/latency) with sensitive fields redacted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa web` starts successfully behind `--features web`.
- `/health` responds 200 with a tiny JSON body.
- `wa web --port 0` works (critical for tests).


## Testing
- Integration tests:
  - Start server on `--port 0`, hit `/health`, then shut down.
  - Assert the server binds to localhost by default.
  - Assert logs include the bound address and per-request spans (with no secrets).

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.6.2","title":"Implement read-only endpoints: /panes /events /search (shared query layer)","description":"# Task: Implement read-only web endpoints

## Goal
Expose minimal read-only endpoints for dashboards, while preserving wa’s safety and schema-stability guarantees.

## Endpoints (v0)
- `GET /health`
- `GET /panes`
- `GET /events?unhandled=1&pane_id=...&limit=...`
- `GET /search?q=...&pane_id=...&since=...&limit=...`

## Design constraints
- Reuse the same query/model code as CLI/robot.
  - The web server should not reimplement data access.
- Responses are JSON with stable schemas and stable ordering.
- Apply the same redaction pipeline as robot/audit/export.

## Pagination & limits
- All list endpoints must be bounded.
  - enforce default and max `limit`
  - provide stable ordering (e.g., newest-first for events)

## Error handling
- Return structured JSON errors with stable error codes.
- Never leak internal DB paths or sensitive config in errors.

## Testing
- Covered by the web server tests bead (`wa-nu4.3.6.4`):
  - schema parseability
  - redaction
  - bind defaults

## Acceptance Criteria
- Endpoints return data when watcher is running.
- Responses remain schema-stable and redacted.
- List endpoints enforce sane defaults and max limits.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.6.3","title":"Web server hardening: localhost-only default, explicit bind, no secret leakage","description":"# Task: Web server hardening (safe-by-default)

## Goal
Ensure the optional `wa web` server is safe-by-default and hard to misuse.

## Threat model
Even “read-only” endpoints can leak:
- terminal transcripts (may contain secrets)
- tokens embedded in URLs
- hostnames, paths, and other sensitive metadata

So the web server must inherit wa’s redaction and safety conventions.

## Requirements
- **Bind defaults**
  - default bind: `127.0.0.1`
  - require an explicit, scary flag to bind publicly (proposed): `--dangerous-bind-any`
  - when binding non-localhost, print a prominent warning

- **No secret leakage**
  - all responses must be redacted using the same redaction pipeline as robot/audit/export
  - never include raw segment bytes by default

- **Hard limits**
  - request size limits
  - sensible default pagination limits

- **Surface area**
  - only GET endpoints in v0
  - no file-serving
  - no CORS-by-default (unless explicitly enabled)

## Testing strategy
- `cargo test --features web` includes cases that assert:
  - localhost bind is the default
  - public bind requires `--dangerous-bind-any`
  - redaction is applied to responses for any field that can contain secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- A naive `wa web` run is not remotely reachable by default.
- Redaction is applied consistently (web == robot == audit == export).
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:29:49.631105886Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T13:04:50.892774706Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-security","area-web","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.6.3","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:40Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.6.3","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:40Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.6.3","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:40Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.6.4","title":"Web server tests: /health + basic endpoint contract (feature web)","description":"# Task: Web server tests (feature web)

## Goal
Prevent the optional web server from silently rotting.

The web server is not on the critical path, but when enabled it must be reliable, safe, and schema-stable.

## Test scope
- Runs under `cargo test --features web`.
- Fully offline (no real WezTerm required).

## Required test cases
- Lifecycle:
  - start server on `--port 0`
  - hit `/health`
  - shut down cleanly

- Contract:
  - `/health` schema is parseable and stable
  - `/panes`, `/events`, `/search` responses are:
    - valid JSON
    - stable ordering where applicable
    - redacted (no raw secrets)

- Safety:
  - default bind is localhost
  - public bind requires explicit `--dangerous-bind-any`

## Observability in tests
- Capture request logs/spans and assert a few key fields exist (method/path/status/latency).
- Assert that sensitive values do not appear in logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --features web` includes at least:
  - `/health` responds
  - endpoint response bodies are schema-parseable
  - redaction + bind-default invariants are enforced


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add a negative test that attempts `--dangerous-bind-any` without explicit confirmation (if applicable) and ensure it is rejected.
  - Add an assertion that there are no write/mutation endpoints registered.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.7","title":"[EPIC] Optional TUI (charmed_rust): pane picker + event feed + transcript viewer","description":"# [EPIC] Interactive TUI Dashboard

## Mission
Transform wa from an invisible background daemon into a **visible control center** with a real-time dashboard showing panes, events, workflows, and health metrics.

## Why This Is Transformative
Terminal automation is inherently opaque. Users cannot see what wa is doing. The TUI changes this fundamentally:

**Before TUI:**
- "I hope it is working"
- "I need to check logs to see what happened"
- "I do not know if the workflow succeeded"

**After TUI:**
- "I can watch everything happening in real-time"
- "I see the workflow progressing step by step"
- "I know immediately when something needs attention"

## TUI Layout Vision
```
┌─ WA Dashboard ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Panes (4 observed, 1 ignored)                                        │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● Pane 1 [codex]     Idle ▪ PromptActive    │ Last: 2m ago          │
│ ● Pane 3 [claude]    Running ▪ CommandActive │ Last: 5s ago          │
│ ○ Pane 5 [local]     Ignored (exclude rule) │                        │
│ ● Pane 7 [gemini]    Idle ▪ PromptActive    │ Last: 30s ago         │
│ ⚠ Pane 9 [codex]     UsageLimitReached      │ Last: 1s ago          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Events (recent)                                              [e] more │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 14:32:01 ⚠ codex.usage_limit_reached    Pane 9  → workflow started  │
│ 14:31:45 ○ session.compaction           Pane 3  → handled           │
│ 14:30:12 ○ codex.session_summary        Pane 1  → no workflow       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Workflows                                                            │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ▶ handle_usage_limits (Pane 9)  Step 3/7: Selecting account...      │
│   [████████░░░░░░░░░░░░] 42%                                         │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Health: ● OK  │ Queue: 0  │ Lag: 12ms  │ DB: 1.2GB                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
[q]uit [p]anes [e]vents [w]orkflows [t]imeline [s]earch [h]elp
```

## Core Views

### 1. Panes Panel
- List all panes with state indicators
- Color-coded status (green=healthy, yellow=warning, red=error)
- Filter: observed/ignored, by agent type
- Select to inspect: show recent output tail, events, actions

### 2. Events Feed
- Real-time event stream
- Severity coloring (info/warning/error)
- Filter: unhandled, by type, by pane
- Select to view details and quick-fix suggestions

### 3. Workflow Progress
- Active workflows with step-by-step progress
- Progress bars and estimated completion
- Expand to see step logs
- Cancel button for stuck workflows

### 4. Health Metrics
- Overall system status
- Queue depths and lag
- DB size and write status
- Circuit breaker states

### 5. Timeline View
- Interactive scrollable timeline
- Cross-pane event correlation
- Zoom in/out on time ranges

### 6. Search/Transcript View
- FTS query interface
- Snippet display with highlighting
- Navigate to context around matches

## Keyboard Navigation
```
Tab / Shift-Tab : Move between panels
Arrow keys      : Navigate within panel
Enter           : Select/expand item
Escape          : Back / collapse
q               : Quit
?               : Help
```

## Implementation

### Framework
Use `ratatui` (successor to tui-rs) with `crossterm` backend:
- Cross-platform (macOS, Linux, Windows)
- Full Unicode support
- Mouse support (optional)

### Architecture
```
wa tui
  ├── App (main loop)
  │     ├── EventBus subscriber (real-time updates)
  │     └── Query client (same as CLI)
  ├── Widgets
  │     ├── PanesPanel
  │     ├── EventsPanel
  │     ├── WorkflowsPanel
  │     ├── HealthPanel
  │     ├── TimelineView
  │     └── SearchView
  └── State management (Redux-like)
```

### Feature Flag
`--features tui` to keep binary size down for headless deployments.

## Testing
- Unit tests: Widget rendering with mock data
- State tests: Verify state transitions
- Integration tests: Event subscription works
- Manual tests: Keyboard navigation, responsiveness

## Success Criteria
- TUI launches with `wa tui` and shows real-time dashboard
- All panels update in real-time as events occur
- Keyboard navigation is intuitive and documented
- Performance: <50ms render time, <100MB memory
- Accessibility: Works with screen readers (basic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- TUI launches reliably behind feature flag.
- Pane picker, event feed, and transcript viewer work with live data.
- Workflow and health panels render without crashes.
